title: Richly Spiced Ethiopian Yacon Stir-fry
description: A rich and creamy meal-prep dish featuring jamaican curry powder, perfect for a gluten-free dinner option.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Approximately one-third of a bulb for minced amount

ingredients.name: Coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Full-fat coconut milk is preferred for richer flavor

ingredients.name: Chicken or vegetable stock
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's gluten-free if using chicken stock

ingredients.name: Peanuts or almonds
ingredients.notes: Optional garnish for texture and flavor

ingredients.name: Thai basil leaves (optional)
ingredients.notes: Fresh Thai basil adds a unique touch of aroma and color

instructions: Heat a bit of coconut oil in a wok or large pan over medium heat.
Add the finely chopped onions, sauté until translucent.
Then add minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute more, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in jamaican curry powder until fragrant and well combined with onions and garlic.
Gradually pour in chicken or vegetable stock, ensuring the mixture coats the bottom of the pan. Let it simmer for a few minutes.
Add jaggery (or sugar), bring to a boil and let reduce slightly while stirring frequently to avoid burning.
Once the consistency becomes thicker and syrupy-like, add finely chopped Yacon root.
Stir well until the Yacon is coated with sauce and softened. The cooking time can be up to 10 minutes for optimal texture.
Add a splash of coconut milk to help thicken the sauce further if needed. Simmer gently for another 2-3 minutes, adjusting seasonings as necessary.
If desired, top the dish with toasted peanuts or almonds and fresh Thai basil leaves just before serving.
Serve hot with steamed vegetables or brown rice for a complete meal.
